findings.description: Center alignment of title text is lost.
findings.evidence: The PDF centers all text on the title slide, whereas the HTML aligns all text to the left.

findings.description: Large broken image placeholder or rendering artifact.
findings.evidence: A massive solid black rectangle appears in the HTML between the introductory text and the data table. This box does not exist in the source PDF.

findings.description: Complex table headers are flattened and merged cells are split.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, header cells like 'Year 1' span multiple sub-columns (Events, Total Days). In the HTML, this hierarchy is flattened into a single row, concatenating the text into individual column names like 'Year 1 Events' and 'Year 1 Total Days Per Month'.

findings.description: Flowchart diagram is missing and replaced by text.
findings.evidence: The PDF contains a visual flowchart diagram with boxes and connecting arrows ('Sound Ecological Environment'). The HTML completely fails to render this as an image, instead replacing the entire diagram with a nested, bulleted text list.

findings.description: Layout of the flowchart diagram is lost.
findings.evidence: The horizontal branching layout of the flowchart in the PDF is completely lost, converted into a vertical bulleted list in the HTML.

findings.description: Content duplication on Page 8: The table 'Monthly Base Flow (cfs) Releases' is included both as an image and as a parsed HTML table.
findings.evidence: In the HTML under section id='page-8', there is an image tag `` immediately followed by the actual parsed `` containing the same data (Tier A, Tier B, etc.).
